Title: Cross-family transcription factor interaction between MYC2 and GBFs modulates terpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for CrMYC2 overexpression; CaMV 35S (constitutive) for CrGBF1 RNAi; reporter promoters tested: ORCA3, BIS1, TDC, STR, G10H
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CrMYC2 alone is not a suitable single-gene engineering target for boosting TIA accumulation in hairy roots because overexpression induces repressors (CrGBF1/2, ZCTs) and lowers alkaloid output. If used, it should be combined with repression-resistant promoter engineering, simultaneous knockout/attenuation of antagonistic repressors, or balanced stacking with ORCA/BIS modules under inducible or tissue-specific control to avoid counter-regulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of CrMYC2 in hairy roots represses many TIA pathway genes and reduces catharanthine, tabersonine, and ajmalicine accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy root overexpression, RNA-seq, qRT-PCR, protoplast transient transactivation assays, yeast two-hybrid, BiFC, LC-ESI-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 µM methyl jasmonate treatment caused biphasic then strong 24 h induction; hairy roots cultured in dark
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots; methyl jasmonate responsive with reduced transcript at 6–12 h and strong induction at 24 h
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; methyl jasmonate responsive with reduced transcript at 6–12 h and strong induction at 24 h
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of CrMYC2-OX and EV hairy roots (accession not provided in paper); co-expression analysis using SRA030483 transcriptomes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite being a known activator of ORCA3/TDC in other contexts, constitutive overexpression in hairy roots triggered strong repressor induction and net pathway suppression, revealing context-dependent control and buffering in TIA metabolism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced TIA accumulation; likely risk of unfavorable regulatory imbalance when overexpressed alone
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; jasmonate-responsive MYC2-type regulator
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYC2 overexpression in C. roseus hairy roots increased CrGBF1/2 and ZCT repressor expression while repressing many TIA biosynthetic genes. 2. CrMYC2 physically interacts with CrGBF1 and CrGBF2 in plant cells. 3. CrMYC2 overexpression significantly reduced catharanthine, tabersonine, and ajmalicine accumulation, indicating buffered or antagonized activation in vivo.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related specialized metabolites; medicinal alkaloids including anticancer and pharmacologically active compounds

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) RNAi construct for suppression; reporter promoters tested: ORCA3, BIS1, TDC, STR, G10H
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CrGBF1 should not be simply silenced to improve TIAs because RNAi reduced alkaloid accumulation, likely through network-wide down-regulation of positive regulators. Engineering should instead consider partial tuning, promoter editing of specific T/G-box sites, or combinatorial manipulation with CrMYC2/ORCA/BIS regulators.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CrGBF1 acts as a repressor on multiple TIA promoters, but its RNAi suppression in hairy roots unexpectedly reduced catharanthine, tabersonine, and ajmalicine due to broader regulatory network effects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi hairy roots, qRT-PCR, promoter-LUC transient assays, site-directed mutagenesis of T/G-box, yeast two-hybrid, BiFC, LC-ESI-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate responsive; roots show highest expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots; methyl jasmonate responsive with strong induction at 24 h
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; methyl jasmonate responsive with strong induction at 24 h
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis using SRA030483; targeted qRT-PCR in RNAi and overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrGBF1 binds T/G-box motifs in ORCA3 and TDC promoters; effect of RNAi is not simple derepression because CrMYC2, ORCA3, BIS1/2, CrGBF2, and ZCTs also decrease.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi suppression reduced TIA accumulation, indicating network instability or loss of coordinated regulation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP G-box/T-G-box binding factor with leucine zipper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrGBF1 represses multiple TIA pathway promoters through T/G-box elements. 2. CrGBF1 forms homo- and heterodimers and also interacts with CrMYC2. 3. CrGBF1 RNAi unexpectedly decreased TIA alkaloid accumulation, indicating it participates in a balanced regulatory module rather than acting as a simple negative switch.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Reporter promoters tested: ORCA3, BIS1, TDC, STR, G10H
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CrGBF2 is a repressor whose activity likely needs attenuation rather than overexpression if aiming to enhance TIAs. Because it forms heterodimers with CrGBF1 and complexes with CrMYC2, rational engineering could target protein interaction surfaces or promoter cis-elements instead of full knockout.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CrGBF2 represses key TIA regulatory and structural gene promoters and antagonizes CrMYC2-mediated activation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in CrMYC2-OX roots, promoter-LUC transient assays, yeast two-hybrid, BiFC
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate responsive; roots show highest expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots; methyl jasmonate responsive with strong induction at 24 h
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; methyl jasmonate responsive with strong induction at 24 h
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis using SRA030483; transcriptome comparison in CrMYC2-OX versus EV
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrGBF2 showed interaction with CrMYC2 in yeast, and both CrGBF1 and CrGBF2 interacted with CrMYC2 in plant cells; repression strength varied by promoter and dimer composition.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely suppresses TIA flux if overactive; full removal may also perturb network balance
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP G-box/T-G-box binding factor with leucine zipper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrGBF2 is induced in CrMYC2-overexpressing roots and is co-expressed with TIA regulators. 2. CrGBF2 forms homo-/heterodimers and interacts with CrMYC2 to repress promoter activity. 3. CrGBF2 contributes to antagonizing CrMYC2 and fine-tuning TIA pathway output.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TDC promoter (native reporter); mutant mTDC promoter with T/G-box AACGTG changed to AAAATG; regulated in hairy roots under native chromosomal promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TDC is a useful engineering target for increasing indole precursor supply, but promoter context is critical because CrMYC2 activates while CrGBFs repress through the same T/G-box. Promoter editing to preserve activator responsiveness while weakening CrGBF binding may be more effective than simple TF overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates tryptophan to produce tryptamine, the indole precursor for strictosidine and downstream TIAs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TDC expression was reduced in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots; its promoter is activated by CrMYC2 and repressed by CrGBF1/2 via the T/G-box.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR, promoter-LUC transient assays, site-directed promoter mutagenesis, prior binding evidence cited, metabolite correlation in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Jasmonate-responsive TIA pathway context in roots/hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in TIA-producing roots/hairy roots; responsive to regulatory perturbation by CrMYC2 and CrGBF1
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Regulated by CrMYC2; CrGBF1; CrGBF2; ORCA3; ZCTs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression profiling in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation of the T/G-box abolished CrGBF1-mediated repression in the transient assay, supporting direct cis-element competition.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Upstream precursor enhancement alone may not raise end products if parallel repressors or iridoid branch constraints persist
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pyridoxal phosphate-dependent aromatic amino acid decarboxylase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point but not established as sole rate-limiting step in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TDC transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX roots but increased in CrGBF1-RNAi roots. 2. CrMYC2 activates, whereas CrGBF1/2 repress, the TDC promoter. 3. Repression depends on a T/G-box cis-element shared with CrMYC2-responsive control.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: STR remains a central engineering node because it catalyzes the first committed TIA step. However, its promoter is strongly repressed by CrGBFs, so combining STR activation with reduction of CrGBF-mediated repression or transporter/sequestration support may be required.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses tryptamine and secologanin to form strictosidine, the central committed intermediate of TIAs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: STR expression decreased in CrMYC2-OX roots; promoter strongly repressed by CrGBF1/2, and repression can be modulated by CrMYC2 interactions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR, promoter-LUC transient assays, RNAi/overexpression hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms committed intermediate leading directly to target-product branch
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Jasmonate-regulated TIA pathway context in roots/hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in TIA-producing tissues; responsive to regulator perturbation in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Regulated by CrGBF1; CrGBF2; ORCA3; ZCTs; affected by CrMYC2-GBF interplay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression profiling in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrMYC2 itself did not significantly activate STR in this assay context, but its interaction with CrGBFs attenuated repression by individual GBF homodimers; GBF1/2 heterodimer still repressed strongly.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial committed TIA step after indole-iridoid convergence
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhancing STR without addressing upstream precursor supply or strictosidine detox/sequestration may cause imbalance or toxicity
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuole/apoplast not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STR transcript was reduced in CrMYC2-OX roots and modestly increased in CrGBF1-RNAi roots. 2. CrGBF1 and CrGBF2 strongly repress the STR promoter. 3. CrMYC2 can antagonize repression by individual CrGBFs, indicating regulator-complex-dependent control.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: G10H is an iridoid-branch engineering target for improving secologanin supply, but the paper shows promoter repression by CrGBFs and pathway sensitivity to TF network balance. A stacked strategy with BIS activators and relief of GBF repression may be needed.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates geraniol at the 10-position to form 10-hydroxygeraniol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: G10H transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX roots and its promoter was repressed by CrGBF1/2.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor for secologanin branch and supplies precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in iridoid-producing tissues/hairy roots; repressed in CrMYC2-OX roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression profiling in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains a T/G-box and is repressed by GBFs, linking GBF action to both indole and iridoid branches.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Upstream iridoid enhancement may require coordinated support by CPR and downstream steps
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme/major control point in iridoid branch context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G10H was repressed in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots. 2. CrGBF1 and CrGBF2 repress the G10H promoter in transient assays. 3. The data place GBF repression in the iridoid branch as well as the indole branch.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ASα may support indole precursor entry and could be combined with TDC to improve indole supply, but this study suggests upstream regulators can suppress it indirectly. Best used in multigene engineering rather than alone.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes anthranilate formation from chorismate in tryptophan biosynthesis, supporting indole precursor availability for TIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ASα transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in hairy roots; repressed in CrMYC2-OX
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as indole branch structural gene affected by regulatory perturbation; direct regulation was not mechanistically validated here.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad perturbation of aromatic amino acid metabolism possible if strongly manipulated
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ASα expression decreased in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots. 2. The result indicates suppression extends into upstream indole precursor formation. 3. Direct promoter control by CrGBFs or CrMYC2 was not shown here.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: IRS is a useful iridoid-branch stacking target, but this study indicates it is vulnerable to upstream regulator imbalance; engineering should combine pathway enzymes with balanced TF control.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the reactive intermediate 8-oxogeranial to form the iridoid scaffold intermediate nepetalactol/related iridoid precursor in secologanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IRS transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in hairy roots; repressed in CrMYC2-OX
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct CrGBF promoter targeting was not tested in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux changes may alter monoterpene metabolism broadly
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRS expression was reduced in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. This supports broad repression of the iridoid branch. 3. No direct promoter assay was performed here.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: IO can be included in multigene iridoid-branch enhancement, but isolated manipulation may be insufficient due to TF-mediated repression of the whole branch.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes iridoid intermediates in the secologanin biosynthetic route downstream of iridoid synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IO transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in hairy roots; repressed in CrMYC2-OX
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450-type oxidase assignment in pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely endoplasmic reticulum/microsome, not tested here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IO expression declined in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. The result supports coordinated repression across the iridoid pathway. 3. Direct regulatory targeting was not tested.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: 7-DLH can support secologanin-pathway flux; use in coordinated iridoid-branch stacking rather than stand-alone engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 7-deoxyloganic acid to form loganic acid in the iridoid/secologanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: 7-DLH transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in hairy roots; repressed in CrMYC2-OX
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May not enhance final alkaloids without coordinated downstream steps
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely endoplasmic reticulum/microsome, not tested here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 7-DLH expression was reduced in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. This indicates repression extends through the iridoid route toward secologanin. 3. Direct regulatory assays were not performed.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: 7-DLGT is a candidate for iridoid-pathway stacking but appears sensitive to negative transcriptional feedback; combine with upstream regulator optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates 7-deoxyloganetic acid to produce 7-deoxyloganic acid in the secologanin biosynthetic route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: 7-DLGT transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in hairy roots; repressed in CrMYC2-OX
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Branch imbalance possible if manipulated alone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 7-DLGT was significantly repressed in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. The result supports broad suppression of iridoid biosynthetic flux. 3. Direct promoter targeting was not evaluated.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CPR is an enabling redox partner for multiple P450 steps; useful in pathway reconstitution or enhancement but not sufficient alone. Could be co-expressed with G10H/other P450s in synthetic biology.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers electrons from NADPH to microsomal cytochrome P450 enzymes, supporting multiple oxidative steps in iridoid/TIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CPR transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for pathway oxidative steps / supports precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in hairy roots; repressed in CrMYC2-OX
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Supports P450 enzymes such as G10H and likely others
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a dedicated pathway enzyme but important for TIA oxidative metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad effects on many P450-dependent pathways if manipulated strongly
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced cytochrome P450 catalytic cycle support
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPR transcript decreased in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. This suggests impaired support for P450-dependent iridoid oxidation steps. 3. No direct promoter regulation assay was performed.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GES can be used as an upstream monoterpene entry enzyme, but this paper shows its transcript was not significantly changed, implying downstream regulation may dominate in this context.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to produce geraniol, initiating the monoterpene/iridoid branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GES expression was not significantly altered in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because assayed, but not significantly affected in the main perturbation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Upstream precursor diversion may affect other terpenes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GES transcript was measured but not significantly altered in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. This suggests selective rather than uniform repression across the iridoid pathway. 3. No direct promoter assay was performed.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: 10HGO may be stacked with G10H and downstream iridoid enzymes, but it was not altered here, so it may be less responsive to the CrMYC2-GBF module.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidoreduces 10-hydroxygeraniol/related intermediates toward 10-oxogeranial in the iridoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: 10HGO expression was not significantly altered in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assayed as part of the iridoid pathway; not significantly changed in CrMYC2-OX.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: None specifically identified in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 10HGO transcript was measured but not significantly altered in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. The CrMYC2-GBF module does not uniformly affect all iridoid genes. 3. No direct promoter assay was performed.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ORCA3 promoter (native reporter); mORCA3 with mutated T/G-box; endogenous promoter in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ORCA3 is a strong positive regulator but is itself repressed by CrGBFs and reduced in CrMYC2-OX roots. Effective engineering should combine ORCA3 activation with mitigation of antagonistic GBF/ZCT repression and balanced branch support.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ORCA3 transcript was decreased in CrMYC2-OX roots and in CrGBF1-RNAi roots; its promoter is activated by CrMYC2 and repressed by CrGBF1/2 via a T/G-box.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq, qRT-PCR, promoter-LUC transient assays, site-directed promoter mutagenesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major activator linking jasmonate signaling to downstream indole/TIA genes such as TDC and STR, but its activity is buffered by GBF repressors.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA-responsive TIA regulatory network; root-enriched expression context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with CrMYC2/CrGBFs in roots and TIA-related tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with CrMYC2/CrGBFs in roots and TIA-related tissues
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of a feedforward/feedback-like module where CrMYC2 activates ORCA3 but also induces CrGBFs that repress ORCA3
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Activated by CrMYC2; repressed by CrGBF1; CrGBF2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of TIA biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation of the T/G-box abolished CrGBF1 repression of the ORCA3 promoter; competitive interaction with CrMYC2 is dose-dependent.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alone may be buffered and insufficient to increase TIAs in hairy roots, as discussed by authors
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TDC; STR and other TIA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No complex demonstrated here; promoter-level antagonism with CrGBFs via shared cis-elements and indirect network interactions
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; subject to transcriptional repression by GBFs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TDC and STR; itself is repressed by CrGBF1/2 and positively influenced by CrMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA3 expression decreased in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots despite prior reports of activation, revealing network buffering. 2. CrGBF1/2 repress ORCA3 promoter activity through a T/G-box. 3. ORCA3 sits within a CrMYC2–CrGBF regulatory module that fine-tunes TIA output.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BIS1 is a useful activator for iridoid-branch enhancement, but this paper shows it is repressed by CrGBFs and down-regulated in CrMYC2-OX and CrGBF1-RNAi lines. Engineering should therefore include stabilizing BIS1 expression against repressor feedback.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BIS1 expression decreased in CrMYC2-OX and CrGBF1-RNAi roots; its promoter is repressed by CrGBF1/2.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major activator of the iridoid branch supplying secologanin precursors for TIA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed in TIA-related tissues; root context emphasized
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely in a balancing module where activator induction is opposed by GBF repression
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Repressed by CrGBF1; CrGBF2; co-functionally linked with BIS2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of iridoid branch biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Additive repression by CrGBF1+CrGBF2 was observed on BIS1 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alone may be insufficient to boost TIAs due to compensatory repressors, as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: G10H and other iridoid pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of iridoid genes; subject to transcriptional repression by GBFs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates iridoid branch genes such as G10H; its own promoter is repressed by CrGBF1/2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS1 is suppressed in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots and also declines in CrGBF1-RNAi lines. 2. CrGBF1/2 repress BIS1 promoter activity. 3. BIS1 is part of the broader balanced activator-repressor network governing the iridoid branch.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BIS2 is a partner iridoid-branch activator. It may need co-engineering with BIS1 and protection from compensatory repression for reliable TIA enhancement.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BIS2 expression decreased in CrMYC2-OX and CrGBF1-RNAi roots.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major activator of the iridoid branch contributing precursor supply for TIAs.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely part of activator-repressor balancing network
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of iridoid branch biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct promoter-LUC assay in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation alone may not raise TIAs because of counteracting repressors
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates iridoid pathway genes; transcript reduced under regulatory perturbation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS2 expression was reduced in CrMYC2-OX and CrGBF1-RNAi roots. 2. This supports broad repression of positive regulatory capacity for the iridoid branch. 3. Direct promoter targeting was not shown in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ZCT repressors should be considered when engineering TIA production because CrMYC2 overexpression induces them. Silencing one regulator may be insufficient unless the wider repressor network is addressed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZCT1, ZCT2, and ZCT3 transcripts were induced in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots and reduced in CrGBF1-RNAi roots, consistent with a repressor network associated with lower TIA output.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulators that repress TIA genes such as TDC and STR and buffer pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Present in TIA-related tissues; induced under CrMYC2 overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely negative-feedback buffering within jasmonate-responsive TIA regulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of TIA biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Known from previous work to bind TDC and STR promoters; in this paper they were monitored as repressor outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Repressor induction limits metabolic engineering gains
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZCT1 was significantly induced in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots. 2. Its expression declined in CrGBF1-RNAi roots. 3. ZCT repressors are part of the broader buffering network limiting TIA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate-related signaling; induced downstream of CrMYC2 overexpression

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ZCT2 should be considered in multi-repressor editing strategies when seeking durable TIA enhancement.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZCT2 transcript was induced in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots and reduced in CrGBF1-RNAi roots.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator contributing to suppression of TIA biosynthetic gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in TIA-related tissues; induced in CrMYC2-OX
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely part of negative feedback buffering
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of TIA biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a measured repressor output; direct assays not performed here.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TDC; STR and related TIA genes (based on family knowledge cited)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZCT2 expression increased in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. ZCT2 decreased in CrGBF1-RNAi roots. 3. The result supports induction of multiple repressor arms when CrMYC2 is overexpressed.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ZCT3 is another candidate for combinatorial repression relief in TIA engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZCT3 transcript was induced in CrMYC2-OX hairy roots and reduced in CrGBF1-RNAi roots.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator contributing to suppression of TIA biosynthetic gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in TIA-related tissues; induced in CrMYC2-OX
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely part of negative feedback buffering
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of TIA biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in CrMYC2-OX versus EV; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a measured repressor output; direct assays not performed here.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TDC; STR and related TIA genes (based on cited background)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZCT3 expression increased in CrMYC2-OX roots. 2. ZCT3 decreased in CrGBF1-RNAi roots. 3. Multiple repressors together likely buffer TIA overproduction.
